title: yosys does not support local variables as loop iterator
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

```systemverilog
`timescale 1us/1us

module test(aclk, in, out);
    input bit aclk;
    input bit in;
    output bit out;
    parameter DEPTH = 3;

    bit flipflop[DEPTH:0];

    assign flipflop[0] = in;
    assign out = flipflop[DEPTH];

    always_ff @(posedge aclk) begin
        integer i;
        for (i = 1; i < DEPTH; i++) begin
            flipflop[i] <= flipflop[i -1];
        end
    end
endmodule
```

```ini
[options]
mode prove

[engines]
smtbmc

[script]
read -formal -sv test.sv
prep -top test

[files]
test.sv
```

## Expected behavior

The loop works as it does in verilator and other tools:

```
$ verilator -Wall test.sv  --cc
$
```

For that matter they support:

```
for (integer i = 1; i < DEPTH; i++)
```

## Actual behavior

```
SBY 22:01:42 [test] base: Warning: wire '\i' is assigned in a block at test.sv:16.14-16.19.
SBY 22:01:42 [test] base: Warning: wire '\i' is assigned in a block at test.sv:16.32-16.35.
SBY 22:01:42 [test] base: test.sv:16: ERROR: Left hand side of 1st expression of procedural for-loop is not a register!
```

SBY 22:01:42 [test] base: test.sv:16: ERROR: Left hand side of 1st expression of procedural for-loop is not a register!
